title: Serving Static Files
content: ### Static File Folder Flask automatically serves files from the `static/` folder at the application root: ``` myapp/ ├── static/ │ ├── css/ │ │ └── style.css │ ├── js/ │ │ └── app.js │ └── images/ │ └── logo.png └── app.py ``` ### Generating Static URLs with `url_for` Use `url_for('static', filename='...')` to generate URLs: ```python from flask import url_for # In Python logo_url = url_for('static', filename='images/logo.png') ``` ```html ``` ### Custom Static Folder You can configure a different static folder: ```python app = Flask(__name__, static_folder="assets", static_url_path="/assets") ``` ### Cache Busting To prevent browsers from using cached versions of static files after updates, append a version parameter: ```html ``` Or use a dynamic version from your app config: ```python @app.context_processor def inject_global_vars(): return {"static_version": "1.0.3"} # In template: # {{ url_for('static', filename='css/style.css') }}?v={{ static_version }} ``` ### Serving Uploads For user-uploaded files, configure a dedicated upload folder: ```python import os from flask import send_from_directory UPLOAD_FOLDER = "uploads" app.config["UPLOAD_FOLDER"] = UPLOAD_FOLDER @app.route("/uploads/") def uploaded_file(filename): return send_from_directory(app.config["UPLOAD_FOLDER"], filename) ```
codeExample:
from flask import Flask, url_for app = Flask(__name__) @app.route("/") def home(): return f'''

Welcome!

'''

quiz.question: Which function generates URLs for static files in Flask?
What is the default folder name for serving static files in Flask?
